heading: Capitol Police Buildings and Grounds Account
label: 1865 Capitol Police Buildings and Grounds Account
notes: CodificationSection was classified to section 166 of former Title 40, prior to the enactment of Title 40, Public Buildings, Property, and Works, by , , .lPub. L. 107–217, § 1116 Stat. 1062Aug. 21, 2002

Amendments2018—Subsecs. (b) to (d). added subsec. (c), redesignated former subsec. (c) as (d), and amended subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, text of subsec. (b) read as follows: “Funds in the account shall be used by the Architect of the Capitol for all necessary expenses for the maintenance, care, and operation of buildings and grounds of the United States Capitol Police.”Pub. L. 115–244

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: There is hereby established in the Treasury of the United States an account for the Architect of the Capitol to be known as “Capitol Police Buildings and Grounds” (hereinafter in this section referred to as the “account”).
resilience and security programs of the Architect of the Capitol; and

the maintenance, care, and operation of buildings, grounds, and security enhancements for facilities of the United States Capitol Police and for other facilities associated with such resilience and security programs at any location.

children.heading: Approval of transfers of appropriations from Legislative Branch agencies
children.label: c Approval of transfers of appropriations from Legislative Branch agencies
A
children.text: the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives, in the case of a transfer from an office of the House of Representatives;

children.label: B
children.text: the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate, in the case of a transfer from an office of the Senate; or

children.label: C
children.text: the Committees on Appropriations of the House of Representatives and the Senate, in the case of a transfer from any other office of the Government.
Amounts transferred under this subsection shall be merged with the account and made available under this section.
This subsection shall apply with respect to fiscal year 2019 and each succeeding fiscal year.

children.heading: Effective date; transfer of funds
children.label: d Effective date; transfer of funds
children.text: This section shall apply with respect to fiscal year 2002 and each succeeding fiscal year. Any amounts provided to the Architect of the Capitol prior to , for the maintenance, care, and operation of buildings of the United States Capitol Police during fiscal year 2002 shall be transferred to the account.August 2, 2002
